questions.q: Who was Bharata's mother?
questions.explanation: Bharata was the son of King Dasharatha and his second queen, Kaikeyi.

questions.q: Who was Bharata's father?
questions.explanation: Bharata was a son of King Dasharatha, the ruler of Ayodhya.

questions.q: Bharata is the brother of which hero of the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: Bharata was the younger half-brother of Rama, the central hero of the Ramayana.

questions.q: What is Bharata best remembered for doing with the throne of Ayodhya?
options: Ruling it as king for himself
Refusing it and ruling in Rama's name
questions.explanation: Bharata refused the throne won by his mother's scheme and ruled only as Rama's steward.

questions.q: What did Bharata place upon the throne to represent the true king?
questions.explanation: Bharata placed Rama's wooden sandals (paduka) on the throne and ruled as a regent in his name.

questions.q: Which kingdom did Bharata govern in Rama's absence?
questions.explanation: Bharata governed the kingdom of Ayodhya while Rama was in exile.

questions.q: For how long was Rama exiled, the period during which Bharata served as regent?
questions.explanation: Rama was exiled for fourteen years, and Bharata ruled as steward for that entire time.

questions.q: Which brother was Bharata's closest and constant companion?
questions.explanation: Shatrughna, Sumitra's son, was Bharata's devoted companion, just as Lakshmana served Rama.

questions.q: How did Bharata feel about his mother's scheme to win him the throne?
questions.explanation: Bharata was horrified and condemned his mother Kaikeyi for exiling Rama on his behalf.

questions.q: Where was Bharata when Rama was sent into exile and Dasharatha died?
options: Away at his maternal uncle's home
questions.explanation: Bharata was visiting his maternal grandfather's kingdom and was absent during these events.

questions.q: What was the name of Bharata's wife?
questions.explanation: Bharata married Mandavi, a cousin of Sita, in the group wedding of Dasharatha's sons at Mithila.

questions.q: To which kingdom did Bharata travel, summoned home after Dasharatha's death?
questions.explanation: Bharata was at the Kekaya kingdom, the realm of his maternal grandfather King Ashwapati.

questions.q: Whom did Bharata blame and harshly rebuke for Rama's exile and his father's death?
questions.explanation: Bharata bitterly denounced his mother Kaikeyi, disowning her actions and her boons.

questions.q: Where did Bharata go to plead with Rama to return and take the throne?
questions.explanation: Bharata led a procession to Chitrakuta, where Rama was living in exile, to beg him to return.

questions.q: How did Rama respond to Bharata's plea to abandon the exile and rule?
options: He refused, honoring their father's word
He fled deeper into the forest
questions.explanation: Rama declined, insisting he must keep his father's vow and complete the fourteen years of exile.

questions.q: After receiving Rama's sandals, where did Bharata choose to live while serving as regent?
options: In the royal palace of Ayodhya
questions.explanation: Bharata lived austerely at Nandigrama, outside Ayodhya, ruling in Rama's name from there.

questions.q: How did Bharata live during the years he ruled as regent?
options: As an ascetic, like an exile himself
questions.explanation: Bharata adopted an ascetic life of matted hair and bark garments, sharing his brother's hardship in spirit.

questions.q: Whom did Bharata wish to install as the rightful ruler of Ayodhya?
questions.explanation: Bharata never wanted the crown for himself and insisted that only Rama was the lawful king.

questions.q: Which sandals did Bharata enthrone, and what did they symbolize?
options: His own, to mark his rule
Rama's, to mark Rama as the true sovereign
Dasharatha's, to honor the dead king
questions.explanation: Rama's sandals on the throne signified that Rama remained king and Bharata governed only on his behalf.

questions.q: What vow did Bharata reportedly make if Rama did not return on the appointed day?
options: To enter a fire and end his life
questions.explanation: Tradition holds Bharata vowed to immolate himself if Rama failed to return when the exile ended.

questions.q: Which devoted messenger flew ahead to assure Bharata that Rama was returning?
questions.explanation: Hanuman went ahead to Nandigrama to bring Bharata the joyful news of Rama's imminent return.

questions.q: How is Bharata generally regarded in the tradition of the Ramayana?
options: As a model of selfless devotion and duty
questions.explanation: Bharata is celebrated as an ideal of brotherly love, renunciation, and dharma.

questions.q: Who was the maternal grandfather of Bharata, at whose kingdom he was staying?
questions.explanation: King Ashwapati of the Kekayas was Kaikeyi's father and thus Bharata's maternal grandfather.

questions.q: Which uncle (Kaikeyi's brother) was associated with Bharata's stay in the Kekaya land?
questions.explanation: Yudhajit, Kaikeyi's brother, was Bharata's maternal uncle, and Bharata had gone to that kingdom.

questions.q: Whom did Bharata perform the funeral rites for upon returning to Ayodhya?
questions.explanation: Bharata returned to find his father Dasharatha dead and performed the king's final rites.

questions.q: Which sage and family priest counseled Bharata and urged him to accept kingship for the people's sake?
questions.explanation: Vasishtha, the royal preceptor of the Ikshvakus, advised Bharata, though Bharata still refused the crown for himself.

questions.q: At whose hermitage was Bharata's great army hosted on the way to Chitrakuta?
questions.explanation: The sage Bharadwaja miraculously feasted Bharata's entire host at his ashram during the journey.

questions.q: When Bharata's vast army approached, why did Lakshmana initially grow suspicious?
options: He feared Bharata came to attack Rama
He thought it was Ravana's army
He believed it was a hunting party
questions.explanation: Lakshmana suspected Bharata might be coming with troops to harm Rama, until Rama reassured him of Bharata's love.

questions.q: Which of Bharata's sons is named in later tradition as founding a city?
questions.explanation: Bharata's sons Taksha and Pushkala are said to have founded Takshashila and Pushkalavati.

questions.q: Into which book (kanda) of the Valmiki Ramayana does Bharata's central role in refusing the throne mainly fall?
questions.explanation: Bharata's refusal of the throne and his pilgrimage to Chitrakuta occur in the Ayodhya Kanda.
